photo_captions: Petworth limewood [?ext] linewood Grinling Gibbons 1690 carvings
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a glued-in colour photograph of ornate wood carvings. The handwritten caption above the photo is partially legible; the text reads something like 'Petworth / limewood / [one word unclear, possibly 'ext' or part of a longer word] / linewood / Grinling / Gibbons / 1690 / carvings'. Petworth House is famous for Grinling Gibbons' limewood carvings. Some caption lines are hard to distinguish from each other.

pages.full_text:
enormous Capability Brown gardens/
pa[r]k, with Timber.
	We tried to see a bit of Salmon's
Bridge Farm, the Campins home that
Simon visited when he was 10, then
continued on to Jane in Churt.
	We had supper & slept at
Longwood House
__Tuesday__
	Simon drove us to the factory,
Ash Vale & Jane & Eve caught the
train to go to the Light box
exhibition on Gertrude Jekyll 1843-1932.
We met up with Julie Aldam for 20
minutes. She pointed us on our walk
to Kate Ruby's House. Simon got there
before us. Kate was there with Dora
& Mylo who slept all the time.
	After lunch we drove Jane back
to Churt & went on to Surs [Jew]
pages.notes: Page continues from a previous page (text begins mid-sentence with 'enormous Capability Brown gardens'). The last word on the page is cut off — reads 'Surs Jew' or possibly 'Surs [illegible]'; could be a place name but is unclear. 'Ash Vale' is a place in Surrey. 'Longwood House' could be a private house or campsite. 'Salmon's Bridge Farm' is mentioned as the Campins family home that Simon visited as a child. The Gertrude Jekyll exhibition is at the 'Light box' (a gallery in Woking, Surrey). No year is directly stated on this page; Surrey locations and the Light Box gallery suggest a date post-2007 (when the Light Box opened), within the volume range 2003-2019. Year left as null. 'Tuesday' is underlined as a day heading.
